option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8761 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8084 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: ai-agent-service config: import: "optional:configserver:http://localhost:8888"
server: port: 8084
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ instance: prefer-ip-address: true instance-id: 127.0.0.1:ai-agent-service:8084 # Forcefully kills ZORO_PC text strings in the metadata registry hostname: 127.0.0.1

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: ai-agent-service config: import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888"
server: port: 8084
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ instance: prefer-ip-address: false instance-id: ai-agent-service:8084 hostname: ai-agent-service

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8761 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8080 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: api-gateway config: import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" # Changed localhost to config-server
server: port: 8080
eureka: client: serviceUrl: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ # Ensure eureka network routing is explicitly declared here too

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: api-gateway config: import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" # Changed localhost to config-server cloud: gateway: routes: - id: ai-agent-service uri: http://ai-agent-service:8084 predicates: - Path=/api/ai/**
server: port: 8080
eureka: client: serviceUrl: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ # Ensure eureka network routing is explicitly declared here too

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8761 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8888 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
version: '3.8'
services: eureka-server: build: ./eureka-server container_name: eureka-server ports: - "8761:8761" networks: - worktrack-network
config-server: build: ./config-server container_name: config-server ports: - "8888:8888" volumes: - /d/projects/worktrack-config-repo:/app/config-repo networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server
employee-service: build: ./employee-service container_name: employee-service ports: - "8081:8081" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host.docker.internal:5432/worktrack_db # CRITICAL CREDENTIAL ENFORCEMENT: - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=postgres - SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=aacini10** extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
task-service: build: ./task-service container_name: task-service ports: - "8082:8082" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host.docker.internal:5432/worktrack_db # CRITICAL CREDENTIAL ENFORCEMENT: - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=postgres - SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=aacini10** extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
leave-service: build: ./leave-service container_name: leave-service ports: - "8083:8083" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host.docker.internal:5432/worktrack_db # CRITICAL CREDENTIAL ENFORCEMENT: - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=postgres - SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=aacini10** extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
ai-agent-service: build: ./ai-agent-service container_name: ai-agent-service ports: - "8084:8084" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - BASE_EMPLOYEE_URL=http://employee-service:8081/api/employees - DIRECT_TASK_SERVICE_URL=http://task-service:8082/api/tasks - BASE_LEAVE_URL=http://leave-service:8083/api/leaves networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
api-gateway: build: ./api-gateway container_name: api-gateway ports: - "8080:8080" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
networks: worktrack-network: driver: bridge

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
version: '3.8'
services: eureka-server: build: ./eureka-server container_name: eureka-server ports: - "8761:8761" networks: - worktrack-network
config-server: build: ./config-server container_name: config-server ports: - "8888:8888" volumes: - D:/projects/worktrack-config-repo:/app/config-repo # Use Windows absolute path so Docker Desktop can mount the host folder. If this fails, try # - //d/projects/worktrack-config-repo:/app/config-repo or use a named volume and copy files at build time. networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server
employee-service: build: ./employee-service container_name: employee-service ports: - "8081:8081" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host.docker.internal:5432/worktrack_db # CRITICAL CREDENTIAL ENFORCEMENT: - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=postgres - SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=aacini10** extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
task-service: build: ./task-service container_name: task-service ports: - "8082:8082" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host.docker.internal:5432/worktrack_db # CRITICAL CREDENTIAL ENFORCEMENT: - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=postgres - SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=aacini10** extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
leave-service: build: ./leave-service container_name: leave-service ports: - "8083:8083" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host.docker.internal:5432/worktrack_db # CRITICAL CREDENTIAL ENFORCEMENT: - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=postgres - SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=aacini10** extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
ai-agent-service: build: ./ai-agent-service container_name: ai-agent-service ports: - "8084:8084" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ - BASE_EMPLOYEE_URL=http://employee-service:8081/api/employees - DIRECT_TASK_SERVICE_URL=http://task-service:8082/api/tasks - BASE_LEAVE_URL=http://leave-service:8083/api/leaves networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
api-gateway: build: ./api-gateway container_name: api-gateway ports: - "8080:8080" environment: - SPRING_CLOUD_CONFIG_URI=http://config-server:8888 - EUREKA_CLIENT_SERVICEURL_DEFAULTZONE=http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ networks: - worktrack-network depends_on: - eureka-server - config-server
networks: worktrack-network: driver: bridge

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8761 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8081 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: employee-service # The unique identity string our Config Server matches to locate settings config: import: "optional:configserver:http://localhost:8888" # Tells Spring Boot to fetch settings from port 8888 first
server: port: 8081 # Locks this specific service container into room number 8081
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/ # Registers this service automatically with Eureka once it finishes booting

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: employee-service # The unique identity string our Config Server matches to locate settings config: import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" # Use Compose service name so containers reach the Config Server
server: port: 8081 # Locks this specific service container into room number 8081
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ # Use Compose service name for Eureka

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
server: port: 8761 # Moves the service registry off default 8080 to standard Eureka port
spring: application: name: eureka-server # Registers this microservice node identification string in the system cluster
eureka: instance: hostname: localhost # Declares the host address location for this instance node client: register-with-eureka: false # CRITICAL: Prevents the server from trying to register with itself as a client fetch-registry: false # CRITICAL: Prevents the server from trying to download a local copy of registry cache tables service-url: defaultZone: http://${eureka.instance.hostname}:${server.port}/eureka/ # Defines the local address endpoint for all client lookup requests

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
server: port: 8761 # Moves the service registry off default 8080 to standard Eureka port
spring: application: name: eureka-server # Registers this microservice node identification string in the system cluster
eureka: instance: hostname: eureka-server # Use the Docker Compose service name so other containers can resolve the registry client: register-with-eureka: false # CRITICAL: Prevents the server from trying to register with itself as a client fetch-registry: false # CRITICAL: Prevents the server from trying to download a local copy of registry cache tables service-url: defaultZone: http://${eureka.instance.hostname}:${server.port}/eureka/ # Defines the local address endpoint for all client lookup requests

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8761 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8083 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: leave-service # The name registered inside the Eureka phonebook config: import: "optional:configserver:http://localhost:8888" # Pulls global database configs from config-server
server: port: 8083 # Claims port room 8083 for this container instance
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/ # Service registration coordinate

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: leave-service # The name registered inside the Eureka phonebook config: import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" # Use Compose service name so containers reach the Config Server
server: port: 8083 # Claims port room 8083 for this container instance
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ # Use Compose service name for Eureka

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8761 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Step 1: Use a lightweight OpenJDK runtime base image FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Step 2: Set the internal working directory inside the container space WORKDIR /app
# Step 3: Copy the compiled jar from your local target build folder into the image COPY target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8082 # Step 4: Run the Java application when the container fires up ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: task-service # Registers its identity inside the cluster mesh config: import: "optional:configserver:http://localhost:8888" # Pulls global database properties from config-server
server: port: 8082 # Tells this microservice to listen on port 8082
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://localhost:8761/eureka/ # Announces its availability to the Eureka dashboard

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring: application: name: task-service # Registers its identity inside the cluster mesh config: import: "optional:configserver:http://config-server:8888" # Use Compose service name so containers reach the Config Server
server: port: 8082 # Tells this microservice to listen on port 8082
eureka: client: service-url: defaultZone: http://eureka-server:8761/eureka/ # Use Compose service name for Eureka
